tools.name: GitHub MCP Server
tools.summary: Official GitHub MCP server for repository, issue, pull request, workflow, project, org, and gist access from AI agents.

install.display: codex mcp add github --url https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ --bearer-token-env-var GITHUB_PAT_TOKEN

methods.command: codex mcp add github --url https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ --bearer-token-env-var GITHUB_PAT_TOKEN

practical.agent_use: Let an agent inspect and update GitHub repository, issue, pull request, workflow, project, org, or gist state when the task explicitly needs GitHub context.
practical.human_use: Use through an agent when GitHub state needs exact readback or controlled repository actions.
practical.cautious_start: Start with the narrowest available token scope or read-only toolset before allowing write operations.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for exact REST or GraphQL fallback and auditable commands.
Combine GitHub state with project-scoped code navigation.
Fetch current docs before changing dependency-sensitive code.

tools.notes: Use targeted toolsets or read-only mode when broad write access is unnecessary.

tools.name: GitHub CLI
tools.summary: Official GitHub command line interface for issues, pull requests, Actions, releases, rulesets, API calls, and authenticated automation.

practical.agent_use: Use for auditable GitHub operations, exact API calls, and PR/check readback when connector coverage is insufficient.
practical.human_use: Use as the baseline command line for issues, pull requests, Actions, releases, and authenticated automation.
practical.cautious_start: Authenticate with the minimum scopes needed for the current repository or organization task.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for GitHub state and lazygit for local branch and commit work.
Use delta to make gh checkout and local review diffs easier to inspect.
Use gh as an auditable fallback for agent-facing GitHub MCP flows.

tools.notes: Prefer gh api for exact REST or GraphQL operations that are not exposed by a connector or MCP tool; treat gh skill as a gh capability rather than a separate tool entry.

tools.name: gh-dash
tools.summary: Terminal dashboard for GitHub pull requests, issues, custom sections, custom actions, and keyboard-driven triage.

practical.agent_use: Low fit for autonomous agents; treat as a human-facing queue dashboard.
practical.human_use: Use for keyboard-driven PR and issue triage when a terminal dashboard improves review flow.
practical.cautious_start: Install only after reviewing third-party extension trust and terminal UI assumptions for the team.

pairs_with.reason: gh remains the underlying authenticated GitHub control plane.
Use gh-dash for queues and lazygit for local branch/diff workflows.

tools.notes: Great for human review queues; audit third-party extension trust and Nerd Font/UI assumptions before default rollout.

tools.name: gh-stack
tools.summary: GitHub extension for stacked branches and stacked pull requests, including agent-facing skill guidance.

practical.agent_use: Evaluate only for teams that explicitly run stacked PR workflows with review boundaries.
practical.human_use: Use when a team has adopted stacked pull requests and understands the extension workflow.
practical.cautious_start: Try in a sandbox repository before using it on production branches.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for direct PR readback and fallback operations.

tools.notes: Preview-oriented stacked PR workflow; use only when stacked PRs are enabled and the team's auth model supports the extension.

tools.name: gh-news
tools.summary: GitHub CLI notification TUI for reading, filtering, previewing, muting, snoozing, and archiving GitHub notifications from the terminal.

practical.agent_use: Not an agent default; notifications usually need human prioritization.
practical.human_use: Evaluate for terminal notification review when the team accepts the extension trust model.
practical.cautious_start: Review publisher trust and token-backed notification actions before routine use.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for exact issue and PR actions after notification triage.

tools.notes: Promising notification-focused gh extension; it uses token-backed GitHub notification actions, so keep it in watch until publisher trust and team fit are reviewed.

tools.name: GitHub Agentic Workflows
tools.summary: GitHub CLI extension for defining and running repository agent workflows through GitHub Actions.

practical.agent_use: Evaluate for repository agent workflows only with explicit billing, permission, and review boundaries.
practical.human_use: Use to prototype GitHub Actions-backed agent workflows after operational review.
practical.cautious_start: Start in a sandbox repository with clear workflow permissions and cost boundaries.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for workflow run and PR state readback.
Compare local terminal agent runtime with Actions-backed workflow execution.

tools.notes: Powerful but operationally heavy; treat permissions, billing, guardrails, and workflow safety as first-class review items.

tools.name: GitHub Copilot CLI
tools.summary: GitHub Copilot coding agent for terminal workflows, with configurable permissions and built-in GitHub MCP support.

practical.agent_use: Use as a separate terminal-native agent runtime when the workflow calls for Copilot CLI rather than Codex tooling.
practical.human_use: Use for human-supervised terminal agent tasks with configured permissions.
practical.cautious_start: Review permissions and repository write boundaries before using it on important branches.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for explicit GitHub state verification around agent changes.
Use current docs to reduce stale implementation assumptions.

tools.notes: A separate agent runtime rather than a Codex tool; prefer this standalone CLI over retired legacy gh-copilot workflows.

tools.name: Playwright MCP
tools.summary: Browser automation MCP server for testing and data extraction through Playwright's accessibility-oriented model.

practical.agent_use: Let an agent validate web behavior through Playwright accessibility and browser automation flows.
practical.human_use: Use when browser validation needs reproducible automation rather than manual clicking.
practical.cautious_start: Run against a constrained browser context and non-production target when possible.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh to read back PR checks after browser validation.
Use DevTools-focused diagnostics when Playwright behavior needs deeper inspection.

tools.notes: Prefer constrained browser contexts for agent runs that do not need local user profile state.

tools.name: Chrome DevTools MCP
tools.summary: Chrome DevTools MCP server for navigation, script execution, screenshots, performance, network, and debugging workflows.

install.display: codex mcp add chrome-devtools -- npx -y chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --slim --headless --isolated

methods.command: codex mcp add chrome-devtools -- npx -y chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --slim --headless --isolated

practical.agent_use: Let an agent inspect navigation, screenshots, performance, network, and console behavior in a constrained Chrome context.
practical.human_use: Use for browser debugging when DevTools-level inspection is more useful than a test runner.
practical.cautious_start: Use slim, headless, isolated mode unless user profile state is explicitly required.

pairs_with.reason: Use Playwright for repeatable flows and DevTools MCP for deeper debugging.
Use gh to connect findings to PR and check readback.

tools.notes: The slim isolated mode is a safer default for general coding-agent use.

tools.name: Serena
tools.summary: Semantic code retrieval and editing MCP server for coding agents, backed by language-server style project understanding.

install.display: codex mcp add serena -- serena start-mcp-server --project-from-cwd --context=codex

methods.command: codex mcp add serena -- serena start-mcp-server --project-from-cwd --context=codex

practical.agent_use: Let an agent navigate and edit a repository with project-scoped semantic code understanding.
practical.human_use: Use through an agent when semantic navigation is faster than plain text search.
practical.cautious_start: Start with project-scoped startup from the active repository, not broad filesystem access.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for PR and issue state while Serena handles code context.
Use docs context before editing framework-sensitive code.

tools.notes: Best used with project-scoped startup rather than broad filesystem access.

tools.name: Context7
tools.summary: Documentation MCP server that injects current library docs and examples into agent prompts.

practical.agent_use: Fetch current library docs and examples before implementing against APIs likely to change.
practical.human_use: Use as an agent-assisted documentation source when current API behavior matters.
practical.cautious_start: Use it to support implementation claims, then verify behavior with project tests or official sources.

pairs_with.reason: Combine current docs with project-scoped code navigation.
Use gh to read back PR and CI state after doc-informed changes.

tools.notes: Useful when framework or API behavior is likely to have changed since model training.

tools.name: lean-lsp-mcp
tools.summary: Lean LSP MCP server for proof diagnostics, goal inspection, Loogle-style search, and optional Lean REPL acceleration.

install.display: codex mcp add lean-lsp --env LEAN_PROJECT_PATH=/path/to/lean/project -- uvx lean-lsp-mcp

methods.command: codex mcp add lean-lsp --env LEAN_PROJECT_PATH=/path/to/lean/project -- uvx lean-lsp-mcp

practical.agent_use: Let an agent inspect Lean proof goals, diagnostics, and search in a bound Lean project.
practical.human_use: Use for proof development support when working in Lean repositories.
practical.cautious_start: Bind LEAN_PROJECT_PATH to the active Lean project before exposing proof tooling.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for PR and check readback around proof changes.
Use delta for readable proof diff review.

tools.notes: Strong fit for Lean repositories; scope LEAN_PROJECT_PATH to the active proof project.

tools.name: lazygit
tools.summary: Terminal UI for everyday Git workflows such as staging, committing, rebasing, stashing, branch navigation, and conflict-oriented local review.

practical.agent_use: Medium fit; useful mainly when a human supervises local Git state around agent changes.
practical.human_use: Use for everyday staging, commits, rebases, stashes, branch navigation, and conflict review.
practical.cautious_start: Review staged changes before committing or pushing.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for GitHub PR state and lazygit for local Git operations.
Use delta to make local diff review more readable.

tools.notes: Best default local Git TUI; pair with gh or gh-dash for GitHub pull request and issue workflows.

tools.name: gitui
tools.summary: Fast Rust terminal UI for local Git workflows, including diff, commit, stash, blame, log, and index operations.

practical.agent_use: Low fit for agents; use mainly as a human local Git interface.
practical.human_use: Use as a lightweight Rust TUI alternative for local Git operations.
practical.cautious_start: Keep it as an alternative until it clearly fits the user workflow better than lazygit.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for GitHub state and gitui for local Git state.
Use delta for readable diffs around local TUI work.

tools.notes: Lightweight Rust Git TUI alternative; keep as an alternative rather than the default over lazygit.

tools.name: tig
tools.summary: Mature text-mode interface for browsing Git history, diffs, refs, blame, and repository state in minimal terminal environments.

practical.agent_use: Low fit for agents; use as a simple history and diff browser in constrained terminals.
practical.human_use: Use for browsing history, blame, refs, and diffs in minimal terminal environments.
practical.cautious_start: Use for read-heavy inspection before changing local Git state.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for GitHub operations while tig stays local and read-oriented.
Use delta when richer diff rendering is available.

tools.notes: Good fallback for SSH, server, and low-dependency environments; less guided than newer Git TUIs.

tools.name: gitu
tools.summary: Magit-inspired terminal Git client for hunk and line staging, commits, fixups, rebases, stashes, and local history workflows.

practical.agent_use: Not an agent default; evaluate only for local Git workflows that need Magit-style interaction.
practical.human_use: Evaluate for hunk and line staging when the team wants a Magit-inspired terminal client.
practical.cautious_start: Try on a non-critical branch before adopting it as a daily Git interface.

pairs_with.reason: Use delta for readable diff review while evaluating local Git UX.

tools.notes: Promising Magit-style TUI with narrower adoption than lazygit and gitui; keep in watch until use cases are clearer.

tools.name: delta
tools.summary: Syntax-highlighting pager for Git, diff, grep, ripgrep JSON, and blame output that improves review readability.

practical.agent_use: Use to make diff and review output easier for humans supervising agent changes.
practical.human_use: Use as a pager upgrade for readable Git, diff, grep, ripgrep JSON, and blame output.
practical.cautious_start: Configure as a pager first; it improves review readability without changing GitHub state.

pairs_with.reason: Use delta to review local diffs around gh-driven PR workflows.
Use delta for readable diffs and lazygit for local Git actions.

tools.notes: High-value diff and pager upgrade; improves review readability without replacing Git or GitHub workflows.

tools.name: ghq
tools.summary: Local repository manager that standardizes clone locations and makes multi-host or polyrepo workspaces easier to navigate.

practical.agent_use: Medium fit for agents that need predictable local clone locations across many repositories.
practical.human_use: Use to standardize clone locations and navigate multi-host or polyrepo workspaces.
practical.cautious_start: Treat it as local repository organization, not as GitHub API or PR tooling.

pairs_with.reason: Use gh for GitHub operations after ghq organizes local repositories.
Use lazygit inside repositories managed by ghq.

tools.notes: Useful for polyrepo and multi-host local repository organization; not a GitHub API tool.

tools.name: hub
tools.summary: Legacy command line wrapper that extends Git with GitHub operations but has largely been superseded by the official GitHub CLI.

practical.agent_use: Do not use for new agent workflows; prefer GitHub CLI.
practical.human_use: Keep only as migration context for legacy users moving to gh.
practical.cautious_start: Do not start new GitHub workflows with hub; migrate to gh instead.

tools.notes: Keep only to explain migration pressure toward gh; do not recommend for new GitHub CLI workflows.

starter_kits.title: Agent repo maintenance
starter_kits.purpose: Inspect GitHub state, navigate code, fetch current docs, and prepare supervised repository changes.
starter_kits.caution: Use limited GitHub scopes, project-scoped code tools, and human approval for writes.

starter_kits.title: Agent browser validation
starter_kits.purpose: Validate UI and browser behavior through constrained automation and diagnostics.
starter_kits.caution: Prefer isolated browser contexts and non-production targets before using authenticated profiles.

starter_kits.title: Human daily review
starter_kits.purpose: Review pull requests, inspect diffs, and manage local Git state during everyday maintenance.
starter_kits.caution: Review local diffs before push or merge; gh-dash remains a reviewed extension, not a required default.

starter_kits.title: Human polyrepo work
starter_kits.purpose: Keep many local repositories organized while preserving GitHub operations in gh.
starter_kits.caution: Use ghq for local repository organization only; do not treat it as GitHub API coverage.

starter_kits.title: Proof repo support
starter_kits.purpose: Support Lean proof work with scoped diagnostics, PR readback, and readable diffs.
starter_kits.caution: Bind Lean tooling to the active proof project before exposing diagnostics to agents.

evaluation_kits.title: Agentic workflow evaluation
evaluation_kits.purpose: Evaluate stacked PRs, Actions-backed agent workflows, or a separate terminal agent runtime.
evaluation_kits.caution: Evaluation only: use sandbox repositories, define billing and review boundaries, and avoid default rollout claims.
